message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To estimate COVID-19 and pre-pandemic low respiratory infection (LRI) mortality in children and young people in Mexico.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Material and methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We estimated the percentage of total mortality attributable to COVID-19 (95% confidence intervals; 95%CI) and corresponding estimates for pre-pandemic LRI mortality.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In 2019, LRIs represented 8.6% (95%CI 8.3, 8.9) of deaths in children 0-9 years and 2.0% (95%CI 1.8, 2.3) in those aged 10-19 years. In 2020, corresponding estimates for COVID-19 were 4.4% (95%CI 4.1, 4.6) and 3.7% (95%CI 3.4, 4.1).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Relative to LRI, COVID-19 may be exerting considerable mortality burden, particularly in older children and adolescents.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19 mortality in children and young people in Mexico

reference.article-title: Children and young people remain at low risk of COVID-19 mortality
Challenges of COVID-19 in children in low-and middle-income countries
On the increasing incidence of SARS-CoV-2 in older adolescents and younger adults during the epidemic in Mexico
Estimación del exceso de mortalidad por todas las causas durante la pandemia del Covid-19 en México
